creator: shitman71@hotmail.com
text: The difference between low budget moterboards and enthusiast-boards are the switching frequency and better power-supply in general, the cheap bords only have a low frequency and manually settings doesnt get you much higher. The frequency on some high cost boards was much more of double then of all the boards up to x370 chipset. This is where you will see the most difference in stabilizing your system. Cooling also affects it much. Disabling cores will do nothing, i have programs that crash running only on one single core and i have programs that never crash the system running on all cores. Disbaling boost and reducing cpu-frequency is the only thing that helps.
